text: The hazard ratio for death was 0.825 (95% CI, 0.681 to 1.002; P=0.052) with salmeterol plus fluticasone.
The hazard ratio for the first exacerbation was 0.86 (95% CI, 0.81 to 0.91; P<0.001) with tiotropium.
The hazard ratio for death from any cause was 0.88 (95% CI, 0.74 to 1.04; P=0.137) with fluticasone-vilanterol.
The rate of moderate or severe exacerbations was lower with triple therapy (rate ratio vs fluticasone-vilanterol, 0.85; 95% CI, 0.80 to 0.90; P<0.001).
The rate ratio for moderate or severe exacerbations was 0.76 (95% CI, 0.69 to 0.83; P<0.001) with triple therapy.
The rate of severe asthma exacerbations was 0.52 events per year with dupilumab versus 0.97 with placebo (rate ratio, 0.54; 95% CI, 0.45 to 0.64; P<0.001).
The rate ratio for asthma exacerbations was 0.45 (95% CI, 0.36 to 0.57; P<0.001) with tezepelumab.
The difference in the annual rate of decline in FVC was 107 ml (95% CI, 65 to 149; P<0.001) favoring nintedanib.
The odds ratio for ACR20 response was 2.0 (95% CI 1.4-2.8; p<0.001) with infliximab plus methotrexate versus methotrexate alone.
The hazard ratio for MACE was 1.33 (95% CI, 0.91 to 1.94) with tofacitinib versus TNF inhibitors.
ACR20 response at week 12 was achieved by 71% of patients with upadacitinib versus 36% with placebo (difference, 35 percentage points; 95% CI, 27 to 43; P<0.001).
PASI 90 response was achieved by 87% of patients with ixekizumab versus 7% with placebo (difference 80 percentage points; 95% CI 75-86).
ASAS20 response was achieved by 61% with secukinumab versus 28% with placebo (odds ratio, 3.9; 95% CI, 2.4 to 6.5; P<0.001).
SRI response at week 52 was higher with belimumab 10mg/kg than placebo (58% vs 44%; odds ratio 1.83, 95% CI 1.30-2.59; p=0.0006).
BICLA response was achieved by 47.8% with anifrolumab versus 31.5% with placebo (difference, 16.3 percentage points; 95% CI, 6.3 to 26.3; P=0.001).
